LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EXPRESS CONSOLIDATION, INC. DBA EXPRESS, AND, RANDALL LESHIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT PLEAD "ANY DAMAGE" WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE CONSUMER LEGAL REMEDIES ACT, OR STANDING, UNDER THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW, B&P CODE SECTION 17500.3. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. FACTUAL ISSUE OF TAX-EXEMPT STATUS CANNOT BE CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINED ON DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Apr-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RANDALL LESHIN, and, EXPRESS CONSOLIDATION, INC. Notice of Motion to Quash Service. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. Dismissal of entire action filed on March 30, 2009. (302KOPP)Judge: Quentin Kopp; Court Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR, 4280

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2009 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EXPRESS CONSOLIDATION, INC. DBA EXPRESS, and, RANDALL LESHIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: John C. Brown, Esq., of Redenbacher & Brown, LLP (415) 409-8600; appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Richard G. Elie, Esq. of the Law Offices of Randall L. Leschin, (954) 941-9711, (Appearing thru CourtCall) appearing for Defendant, Washington Express and Randal Leshin; and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend the second Cause of Action. The Complaint does not plead "any damage" within the meaning of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act, or standing, under the Unfair Competition Law, B&P Code section 17500.3. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action. Factual issue of tax-exempt status cannot be conclusively determined on demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

PROOF OF SERVICE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2009 Off Calendar
